ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensor_readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor reading */,
  "time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that took the reading */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the reading was taken */,
  "measurement_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement type */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the measurement */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the measurement */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the reading (e.g., valid, invalid, flagged) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_readings_measurement_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_type_id") REFERENCES sensor_types ("sensor_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_readings_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the sensor is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "last_calibrated_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_sensor_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_type_id") REFERENCES sensor_types ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "sensor_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor type */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Default unit of measurement for the sensor type */,
  "range_min" REAL /* Minimum value the sensor can measure */,
  "range_max" REAL /* Maximum value the sensor can measure */,
  "precision" REAL /* Precision of the sensor */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  "climate_zone" TEXT /* Climate zone of the location */,
  "soil_type" TEXT /* Soil type of the location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, field operator) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Active status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor reading being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the sensor reading */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor reading was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (browser, device) used for access */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access was successful */,
  "data_size" INTEGER /* Size of the data accessed (in bytes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES sensor_readings ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
